name: Clinical Protocol State Machine with Choice Pseudostates and Guards
input: Create a state diagram for a clinical trial patient state machine. Initial state -> Screening. From Screening, choice node: if eligible -> Enrolled, if ineligible -> Screen Failure (terminal). From Enrolled -> Randomized. From Randomized, choice node: Treatment Arm A or Treatment Arm B (random assignment). Both treatment arms -> On Treatment. From On Treatment, choice node based on response assessment: Complete Response -> Follow-Up, Partial Response -> Continue Treatment (loop back to On Treatment), Progressive Disease -> Off Treatment. From Off Treatment -> choice: if adverse event -> Safety Follow-Up -> Study Complete, if disease progression -> Survival Follow-Up -> Study Complete. From Follow-Up after 2 years -> Study Complete (terminal). Show choice pseudostates as diamond/choice nodes with guard conditions on transitions.
qualityCriteria: At least 2 choice pseudostates with branching
Guard conditions on transitions (eligible/ineligible or response types)
Treatment Arm A and Treatment Arm B states present
Loop-back from partial response to On Treatment
Terminal states (Screen Failure, Study Complete) present
At least 12 distinct states or nodes
Follow-Up state with transition to Study Complete
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: stateDiagram-v2 with 14+ states, choice pseudostates, guard conditions, treatment arms, response branching, terminal states.
